channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Deep Dive into Open Agent SDK (Part 6): Multi-LLM Providers and Runtime Controls
item.description: An Agent shouldn't be locked to a single LLM provider. Different tasks suit different models — simple questions use cheap models, complex reasoning uses expensive ones, and some scenarios even...

item.title: Deep Dive into Open Agent SDK (Part 5): Session Persistence and Security
item.description: An Agent is more than a one-shot Q&A tool. A truly useful Agent must do three things: remember context (where we left off), control permissions (which operations are allowed), and audit behavior...

item.title: Deep Dive into Open Agent SDK (Part 4): Multi-Agent Collaboration
item.description: A single Agent, no matter how powerful, is just one executor. Real development tasks are often multi-step and multi-role: someone explores the codebase, someone designs a plan, then someone writes...

item.title: Deep Dive into Open Agent SDK (Part 3): MCP Integration in Practice
item.description: The previous article looked at the SDK's 34 built-in tools — file read/write, Bash execution, code search — covering common development scenarios. But an Agent's capabilities can't rely solely on...

item.title: Deep Dive into Open Agent SDK (Part 2): Behind the 34 Built-in Tools
item.description: The previous article analyzed how the Agent Loop works, including one crucial step: "execute tools." When the LLM says "I need to call Bash," the SDK actually spawns a process to run the command....

item.title: Deep Dive into Open Agent SDK (Part 1): Agent Loop Internals
item.description: Most LLM wrapper libraries do three things: send a request, get a response, done. But a true Agent goes further — it decides whether to call tools, executes them, feeds the results back to the...

item.title: Open Agent SDK (Swift): Build AI Agent Applications with Native Swift Concurrency
item.description: If you're a Swift developer looking to integrate AI Agent capabilities into your macOS apps, the options are slim. Most Agent frameworks are built for Python or TypeScript, leaving the Swift...

item.title: DSA, step by step
item.description: 👉 http://decoded-app.vercel.app/ I built this as a small way to make DSA feel less like memorizing patterns and more like understanding how problems are actually approached. Not just what the...

item.title: How to Undo a Git Commit (The Right Way)
item.description: Searching "how to undo a git commit" returns 10 Stack Overflow answers with different commands and no explanation of which to use when. Here is the decision tree. Step 1: Did you push the commit?...

item.title: I built a journaling app where the AI refuses to talk on Sundays. Day 8/9 of 30.
item.description: Day 8 and 9 of building Evengood — a 60-second journaling app — for the Build with MeDo $50K hackathon. This one took two days because I went the wrong way first. The trap I almost fell into By...

item.title: Google’s new gradient icon design is coming to more apps
item.description: In late 2025, Google started rolling out new icons with a gradient design. Now it seems the new look is coming to the rest of Google's apps. 9to5Google got its hands on images of the new icons...

item.title: Trump turns the WHCD shooting into a pitch for the White House ballroom
item.description: Within hours of an armed gunman's attempt to enter the White House Correspondents Dinner, attended by top administration officials and hundreds of journalists, President Donald Trump did what he...

item.title: Our principles
item.description: Our mission is to ensure that AGI benefits all of humanity. Sam Altman shares five principles that guide our work.

item.title: Tomora’s Come Closer is an ecstatic love letter to 90s dance music
item.description: Before Coachella, Tomora wasn't on my radar at all. It's actually only by chance that I stumbled upon them - I opened the wrong stream because my TV was lagging like a MFer. I paused for a few...

item.title: To buy this Bay Area home, you’ll need Anthropic equity
item.description: Someone’s offering an unusual deal for a 13-acre property in Mill Valley, just north of South Francisco.

item.title: Skylight’s 15-inch smart calendar is down to its lowest price to date
item.description: When you’re juggling more than just your own calendar, staying organized can be overwhelming. Fortunately, the Skylight Calendar 2 can help simplify things by syncing multiple calendars in a...

item.title: After three months on Linux, I don’t miss Windows at all
item.description: In January I finally made good on my threat/promise to install Linux on my desktop. I wanted to see how far I could get using a Linux PC as my main computer without doing a bunch of research...

item.title: The plan to quietly kill Coyote v. Acme blew up in David Zaslav’s face
item.description: This is The Stepback, a weekly newsletter breaking down one essential story from the tech world. For more on Hollywood trends and streaming culture, follow Charles Pulliam-Moore. The Stepback...

item.title: Anthropic created a test marketplace for agent-on-agent commerce
item.description: In a recent experiment, Anthropic created a classified marketplace where AI agents represented both buyers and sellers, striking real deals for real goods and real money.

item.title: Maine’s governor vetoes data center moratorium
item.description: L.D. 307 would have imposed the country’s first statewide moratorium on new data centers — lasting, in this case, until November 1, 2027.

item.title: Trump fires the entire National Science Board
item.description: Multiple sources are reporting that the Trump administration has dismissed the entire National Science Board (NSB). The NSB advises the president and Congress on the National Science Foundation...

item.title: An influx of used EVs could drive down prices
item.description: Part of what has held back electric cars has been the cost. But an influx of used vehicles over the next three years could bring prices down dramatically. In 2025, just 123,000 leases on EVs...

item.title: OpenAI CEO apologizes to Tumbler Ridge community
item.description: In a letter to the residents of Tumbler Ridge, Canada, OpenAI CEO Sam Altman said he is “deeply sorry” that his company failed to alert law enforcement about the suspect in a recent mass shooting.

item.title: Why Cohere is merging with Aleph Alpha
item.description: Canadian AI startup Cohere is taking over Germany-based Aleph Alpha with support from Lidl’s owner, Schwarz Group. With the blessing of their governments, the companies intend to offer a sovereign...

item.title: Researchers say we’re talking less than ever
item.description: Researchers at the University of Missouri-Kansas City and the University of Arizona say that between 2005 and 2019, the number of words we speak out loud to another human being fell by nearly 28...

item.title: Why Tokyo is the most important tech destination of 2026
item.description: SusHi Tech Tokyo 2026 has four tightly defined technology domains, each backed by live demonstrations, dedicated exhibit floors, and sessions featuring the people actually building and funding...

item.title: Apple under Ternus: what comes next for the tech giant’s hardware strategy
item.description: John Ternus, Apple's incoming CEO, is a hardware guy, signaling Apple may be putting devices back at the center of its strategy.

item.title: Sharge’s fast Qi2.2 MagSafe battery is down to $70 with a free USB-C cable
item.description: I’ve been testing compact, magnetic Qi2 power banks that can snap onto your phone for an upcoming buying guide. They make recharging much easier than bringing along a huge battery that weighs down...

item.title: Discord Sleuths Gained Unauthorized Access to Anthropic’s Mythos
item.description: Plus: Spy firms tap into a global telecom weakness to track targets, 500,000 UK health records go up for sale on Alibaba, Apple patches a revealing notification bug, and more.

item.title: Ace the Ping-Pong Robot Can Whup Your Ass
item.description: Ace can read the trajectory of a ball, adjust the racket angle, and respond with strokes that keep the exchange alive with real players.
